spec_match.rationale: Uses real ::part(input):focus-visible, ::part(input):hover, and ::part(label):focus-within host-page rules against a genuine shadow-DOM custom element, while probing the rejected structural chains that still remain outside the ::part() boundary.

interactive.rationale: Real named form fields, keyboard focus, Chrome 130 and pre-Chrome-130 mode buttons, and the selector boundary panel are all testable in-page.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page still matches the feature blurb by demonstrating a host-page focus ring on an exposed input part.

edge_cases.rationale: The page now contrasts pre-130 and Chrome 130 modes, applies the label :focus-within rule, and runs live CSS.supports() probes for combinator, :has(), and :nth-child() chains that remain rejected.

references.rationale: The ChromeStatus reference now points to the canonical feature id 5195333643272192.

design_system.rationale: The existing design-token based page styling remains intact.

summary: Repaired after chrome-devtools-mcp showed :focus-within support was true but the label part did not restyle, and no rejected-chain panel was present. The custom element now wraps each control in the exposed label part so form-field::part(label):focus-within changes the label color, the pre-130 toggle removes all host-page ::part() state rules and uses JS-toggled host classes for the fallback state, and the boundary panel probes accepted focus chains plus rejected combinator/:has()/:nth-child() chains.
